# Ralphglasses Antigravity Rules

Use `AGENTS.md` as the canonical repo instruction file. Use `.agents/workflows/` for reusable operator flows, `.agents/skills/` for canonical skills, and `.mcp.json` for repo-local MCP registration.

## Session

Session management patterns:
- Provider dispatch via `providers.go` ‚Äî each provider has a cmd builder and event normalizer
- Provider enum: `codex` (default, gpt-5.4), `claude` (sonnet), `gemini` (gemini-3.1-pro), `antigravity` (external interactive handoff)
- Budget tracking in `budget.go` ‚Äî per-provider cost enforcement; Codex, Gemini, and Antigravity budgets are tracked externally
- LaunchOptions carries all session config; TeamConfig for multi-session coordination
- Session lifecycle: launch ‚Üí stream ‚Üí terminate; use SIGTERM/SIGSTOP/SIGCONT for process control
- Resume support: Claude, Gemini, Codex, and Cline support resume; Antigravity is launch-only through ralphglasses
- Always return `codedError()` from handlers, never `(nil, error)`

## MCP

MCP server patterns (222 tools across 30 deferred-loaded groups):
- Tool groups registered via `ToolGroupRegistry` / `ToolGroupBuilder` in `registry.go`
- Each group has a `build*Group()` method on `*Server` in domain-specific `tools_builders_*.go` files
- Error handling: always use `codedError(ErrCode, msg)` returning `(*mcp.CallToolResult, nil)`
- Middleware stack: Concurrency(32) ‚Üí Trace ‚Üí Instrumentation ‚Üí EventBus ‚Üí Validation ‚Üí ResponseSizeLimit(4096) ‚Üí Hooks
- Deferred loading: groups load on first access via `ralphglasses_load_tool_group`
- ValidatePath guards required on all handlers accepting file/repo path params (security: path traversal)
- Response size limit is 4KB ‚Äî return summaries with follow-up detail tools for large payloads

## TUI

BubbleTea v2 TUI patterns:
- Styles in own package (`internal/tui/styles/`) to avoid import cycles ‚Äî always import styles from there
- View stack: `CurrentView` + `ViewStack` for breadcrumb navigation
- Views in `views/` sub-package, components in `components/` sub-package
- Theme hot-swap via `styles/hotswap.go` using fsnotify + `tea.Program.Send()`
- `View()` returns `tea.View` (v2 API), not `string`
- View dispatch uses `viewDispatch` registry map (preferred) with switch fallback
- Keymap system via `keymap.go` ‚Äî context-sensitive bindings
- Golden tests in `testdata/` for snapshot regression ‚Äî update goldens when changing view output

## Loop

Loop and process lifecycle:
- `.ralph/status.json` ‚Äî LoopStatus (current loop state, iteration count, budget remaining)
- `.ralph/.circuit_breaker_state` ‚Äî CircuitBreakerState: CLOSED (healthy), HALF_OPEN (testing), OPEN (stopped)
- `.ralph/progress.json` ‚Äî Progress tracking per iteration
- `.ralph/improvement_journal.jsonl` ‚Äî append-only JSONL, one entry per session
- `.ralphrc` ‚Äî shell-style KEY="value" config (sourced by scripts)
- Process management: `os/exec` with process groups (`Setpgid`), SIGTERM for graceful stop, SIGSTOP/SIGCONT for pause/resume
- Reactive updates: fsnotify watches `.ralph/` dirs; falls back to 2s polling if fsnotify unavailable

## Cost

Model routing for ralphglasses workloads:
- Fleet status/discovery: use sonnet (fast, cheap) ‚Äî these are read-only lookups
- Self-improvement/R&D cycles: use opus (deep reasoning needed for code changes)
- Session orchestration: use sonnet unless debugging complex provider interactions
- Subagent model is sonnet globally (CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=sonnet)
- Budget awareness: ralphglasses tracks per-provider costs; check `ralphglasses_session_budget` before long operations
- Compaction at 70% ‚Äî keep critical context near top and bottom of prompts
